case_number: Crl. Misc. Application No. S-298 of 2022

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Crl. Misc. Application No. S-298 of 2022 Crl. Misc. Application No.S-39 of 2024 DATE OF HEARING ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE For hearing of main case. O R D E R. 21.08.2025 Mr. Alam Sher Khan Bozdar advocate for the applicant. Mr. Hidayatullah Balcoh advocate for the private respondents. Mr. Mansoor Ahmed Shaikh, Deputy P.G for the State. _____*******______ The instant application u/s 491 Cr.P.C filed by the applicant alleging therein that his daughter Fahmeeda, aged about 16/17 years was illegally detained by the private respondent Nos.03 to 06. During course of arguments, it has been brought on record that a case bearing Crime No. 111/20225, offence u/s 365-B, 452, 382 PPC at Police Station Airport was registered by the applicant regarding abduction of his daughter Fahmeeda, the alleged abductee in the instant case, at the hands of respondents 03 to 06. Not only this, but respondent Nos.03 to 06 being accused in the above referred case, were facing trial before the competent court of law. According to learned counsel for the private respondents, at the verge of concluding trial of above case, a report was submitted by the police that alleged abductee was done to death. Subsequently, Section 302 PPC and other penal Sections were added and an amended charge was framed against the accused. The learned counsel submits that all the material witnesses have been examined, except three members of Joint Investigation Team and the case is at the verge of concluding trial. Such fact is also affirmed by the learned counsel for the applicant and DPG for the State. In the prevailing circumstances, learned counsel for the applicant was put on notice to satisfy the court, how the instant application under Section 491 Cr.P.C was maintainable, when a Criminal case regarding abduction of the alleged detainee was already under trial before competent Court of law.

Such fact is also affirmed by the learned counsel for the applicant and DPG for the State. In the prevailing circumstances, learned counsel for the applicant was put on notice to satisfy the court, how the instant application under Section 491 Cr.P.C was maintainable, when a Criminal case regarding abduction of the alleged detainee was already under trial before competent Court of law. The learned counsel looking to the circumstances does not press the instant application. Accordingly, both the captioned applications stand dismissed as not pressed. Office is directed to place on record signed copy of this order in captioned connection Crl. Misc. Application. J U D G E
